issue: The manuscript assumes that integrating a GAN framework into diffusion models inherently improves sample quality without addressing potential instability issues.
fix: Conduct ablation studies to isolate the effect of each component (GAN, gradient penalty) on stability and performance.
body: The manuscript assumes that integrating a GAN framework into diffusion models inherently improves sample quality without addressing potential instability issues.

fix: Include comparative experiments with other stabilization techniques and report detailed metrics.
body: The paper claims improved training stability via gradient penalty but does not provide empirical evidence or comparisons with alternative regularization methods.

issue: The manuscript uses unsupported leaps in stating that the GAN-enhanced diffusion model produces more realistic samples without discussing potential overfitting risks.
fix: Perform additional validation on unseen datasets and include regularization strategies to mitigate overfitting.
body: The manuscript uses unsupported leaps in stating that the GAN-enhanced diffusion model produces more realistic samples without discussing potential overfitting risks.
